notes: The engineering layer is consistently additive. The phrase 'In engineering terms' explicitly marks the analog as a register substitution. The verse's own claim (that the embodied passes to another body) is not flattened into the engineering claim; the engineering claim is presented as an analog of the analogical structure, not as the 'true meaning' of the verse. The disagreement on dehāntara-prāptiḥ ('passes into another body at death' vs Shankara's preservation of the analogical-structure-as-argument) signals to the reader that the verse has its own scope not exhausted by the engineering reading.
